_about: SYNTHETIC demo configuration for the novelty-effect analysis framework. All data produced from this config is simulated and clearly labeled. Setting: a large consumer web platform's experimentation program. No real users, metrics, or company data are involved.

data._about: Controls the synthetic data generator. Effects are expressed as absolute fractional lifts on the treatment metric relative to control (0.02 means +2 percent). Each experiment-metric has a KNOWN ground-truth long-term effect L_true, an optional decaying transient of amplitude A_true and time-constant tau_true, a category label, and an optional additive weekly seasonal swing of amplitude seasonal_amp_true on the daily lift.

seasonality._about: A subset of random series carries an additive weekly seasonal swing on the daily lift, S*sin(2*pi*dow/7 + phase), which averages to zero over any whole week but biases a partial-week read. This is what the seasonality floor in the duration guidance protects against.

detector._about: Thresholds for classifying a daily-lift series into flat / novelty_overshoot / primacy_dip / genuine_ramp. tau_grid spans candidate decay constants (days). A series is flagged as a decaying transient (novelty or primacy) only when the transient is statistically significant, practically large, and has visibly saturated inside the observed window.

duration._about: Duration guidance. The decay-based component is how many days until the per-day novelty transient falls within tolerance of the estimated long-term effect. tol_abs is an absolute tolerance in fractional-lift units (0.005 = 0.5pp). The seasonality floor forces at least a whole number of seasonal cycles so a partial-week day-of-week mix cannot dominate the read. The final recommendation is the maximum of the two.

estimator._about: Naive vs debiased comparison. At each decision day K the naive estimate is the cumulative average daily lift over days 0..K-1; the debiased estimate is the fitted asymptote of the transient model. Bootstrap draws parametric resamples of the daily lifts to build a confidence interval on the debiased asymptote.

projection._about: Long-term impact projection for still-climbing genuine ramps. From the fit at the end of the collected window we project the lift at a business horizon (default 365 days), with a bootstrap confidence interval that widens with the horizon. This is an extrapolation beyond the data and is labeled as such.
